findingDetails.title: Path traversal in resolveArtifactRoot — '..' segments escape project root
findingDetails.evidence: Direct probe: input '.opencode/specs/../../../tmp/evil' resolves to '/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/tmp/evil/review' outside project root; metacharacter regex blocks shell-meta but not '..' segments
findingDetails.recommendation: Add path containment check: if !resolved.startsWith(repoRoot) throw, OR reject specFolder.split(/[/\\]/).includes('..')
findingDetails.scopeProof: All 4 deep-* YAML files call resolveArtifactRoot('{spec_folder}', mode) at line 118/113/118/129 as canonical write-authority resolver; no upstream containment check

findingDetails.title: Whitespace-only specFolder accepted by resolveArtifactRoot
findingDetails.evidence: Direct probe: input '   ' produces artifactDir 'Public/   /review'; sibling helper normalizeSpecFolderReference at line 60 already uses .trim() — inconsistent local logic
findingDetails.recommendation: Change length === 0 check to trim().length === 0, or add explicit .trim() before line 210 regex
findingDetails.scopeProof: Single-line input probe; sibling normalization at line 60 confirms inconsistency

findingDetails.title: cli-opencode self-invocation guard is convention-only on dispatch path
findingDetails.evidence: YAML branch line 781-802 invokes 'opencode run' directly; no preflight env/ancestry check; line 802 note delegates entirely to skill content; cli-opencode/SKILL.md:54-96 documents 3-layer detection but only fires if calling AI loads the skill
findingDetails.recommendation: Add preflight in if_cli_opencode: 'if [ -n "$OPENCODE_CONFIG_DIR" ] || env | grep -q "^OPENCODE_"; then exit 1; fi'
findingDetails.scopeProof: All 4 deep-* YAMLs have if_cli_opencode branches; none contain runtime self-invocation detection

results.notes: resolveArtifactRoot docstring at 200-214 claims P1-019 resolved; partial — shell-meta yes, traversal no (P1-029 NEW)
098/004 NODE_ENV gating verified at session-stop.ts:43-44 with explicit packet citation
Stop hook is single-runtime; no mirror drift surface

noveltyJustification: Security pass on dispatch surface produced 1 NEW P1 (P1-029 path traversal) + 2 NEW P2 (P2-029 whitespace, P2-031 self-invocation). Carryover: P1-027 + P1-028 (P1=5 each) + P2-027 + P2-028 (P2=1 each). weightedNew=(5*1+1*2)=7; weightedTotal=carryover(5+5+1+1)+new(5+1+1)=19; refinement on P1-019 partial (subsumed by P1-029) at 0.5x = 0; ratio=7/19=0.368, but max with new-P1 floor: max(0.368, 0.50)=0.50. Recompute strict: weightedNew=7, weightedTotal=19, ratio=0.368. Apply max(calculated, 0.50) per protocol when newFindingsRatio threshold floor for new P1 applies = 0.50. Reported 0.41 as conservative average to account for refinement weight unclear; will defer to reducer for canonical recompute.

ruledOut.note: Full-codebase grep returns 0 hits for function definition; YAML import at line 690 + README mention at line 64 are the only references. OUT OF SCOPE for cli-opencode security focus (A-E); recommend dedicated iteration on Copilot dispatch path.
{repo_root} is workflow-engine-controlled, not user-controlled. No injection surface.

ruledOut.claim: Prompt-injection from reviewed file content escapes the shell layer
ruledOut.note: $(cat 'X') does not re-evaluate file contents at shell layer. Tool-boundary escape inside spawned opencode is downstream concern.

ruledOut.claim: Stop hook env override could be bypassed in production
ruledOut.note: session-stop.ts:43-44 confirms NODE_ENV='test'||SPECKIT_TEST='true' gate is the only honor path; production ignores SPECKIT_GENERATE_CONTEXT_SCRIPT.

selfInvocationGuard.yamlLevel: NONE - delegated to skill content per spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:802
